summary: This week in AI news, major advancements were made in AI models, hardware, and applications. OpenAI launched the ChatGPT for Small Businesses program, while NVIDIA introduced the Vera Rubin for gigascale AI. Google revealed faster and cheaper Gemini models, and Anthropic's $1.5 billion copyright settlement was approved.
insight: The increasing use of AI in various industries, such as music and construction, highlights the need for addressing copyright and IP theft concerns, as seen in Anthropic's settlement and the US threatening sanctions against Chinese AI models.
highlights: OpenAI's ChatGPT for Small Businesses program aims to help entrepreneurs build AI skills and automate work.
NVIDIA's Vera Rubin is designed to maximize intelligence per dollar for post-training workloads, a key metric for agentic AI.
Google's new Gemini models offer faster and cheaper alternatives to large AI security models.

articles.title: Introducing the ChatGPT for small business program
articles.description: OpenAI launches the ChatGPT for Small Businesses program, helping entrepreneurs build AI skills, automate work, and grow with ChatGPT Work.

articles.title: Music streamer Deezer says more than 50% of daily uploads are AI-generated
articles.description: Deezer said more than 90,000 AI-generated tracks were uploaded daily on the platform in June.

articles.description: OpenAI shares lessons from deploying long-running AI models, highlighting new safety risks, observed failures, and improved safeguards through iterative deployment.
